column539: the_sender_score_is_the_percent_sent_in_the_trust_game_where_one_player_sends_money_to_another_the_money_is_multiplied_and_the_second_player_can_return_some_to_the_first_player_the_person_was_asked_to_list_up_to_6_thoughts_that_crossed_their_mind_while_playing_as_the_sender_and_they_answered_"I'm just going to send a little to see what they will do with it". column540: the_sender_score_is_the_percent_sent_in_the_trust_game_where_one_player_sends_money_to_another_the_money_is_multiplied_and_the_second_player_can_return_some_to_the_first_player_the_person_was_asked_to_list_up_to_6_thoughts_that_crossed_their_mind_while_playing_as_the_sender_and_they_answered_"If I send them all of the money, that will maximize the total money.; If in good faith I give them all of the money, and they are aware of this, then I hope they'll also act in good faith by giving me a portion back". The person was then asked to list up to 6 thoughts that crossed their mind while playing as the receiver and they answered: "When I'm recieving money, the other person isn't bound to give me any. So any amount is fine.; If it was their money to begin with, and they're giving me a portion as a gift, I will give them the slightly larger half, if the halves are uneven. "
column541: the_sender_score_is_the_percent_sent_in_the_trust_game_where_one_player_sends_money_to_another_the_money_is_multiplied_and_the_second_player_can_return_some_to_the_first_player_the_person_was_asked_to_list_up_to_6_thoughts_that_crossed_their_mind_while_playing_as_the_sender_and_they_answered_"Is it fair to keep more, or should I split it evenly?; What would the other person do if they were in my position?; If I send more, will they reciprocate fairly?; Keeping more might be rational, but generosity could build trust.; Would I feel guilty if I kept too much?; This is just a one-time decision—does that change my approach?". The person was then asked to list up to 6 thoughts that crossed their mind while playing as the receiver and they answered: "Did they act fairly when they sent the money?; Should I reward their trust, or prioritize my own gain?; If I send more back, will that reflect well on my character?; Keeping more feels tempting, but fairness matters.; Would I regret my decision if the roles were reversed?; What is the most reasonable balance between self-interest and fairness?"
column542: the_sender_score_is_the_percent_sent_in_the_trust_game_where_one_player_sends_money_to_another_the_money_is_multiplied_and_the_second_player_can_return_some_to_the_first_player_the_person_was_asked_to_list_up_to_6_thoughts_that_crossed_their_mind_while_playing_as_the_sender_and_they_answered_"It is important how much the other person cares for me.; I don't want to give it back to someone who satisfies my greed.; I want to have more money than the other person; If the other person returns it to me, I have no choice but to return it to them.". The person was then asked to list up to 6 thoughts that crossed their mind while playing as the receiver and they answered: "I wonder if other people will be satisfied with the amount I returned.; I want to share it with the other person in a friendly way."
